project.title: Ubiquitination of protein Tau in Alzheimer's disease
project.projectDescription: Identification of posttranlational modifications of Tau in Alzheimer's disease
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Proteins were extracted from AD brain, and then protein Tau was enriched with IP and digested with trypsin. The resulting peptides were analyzed with LC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MS raw files were analyzed with MaxQuant software.

references.referenceLine: Puangmalai N, Sengupta U, Bhatt N, Gaikwad S, Montalbano M, Bhuyan A, Garcia S, McAllen S, Sonawane M, Jerez C, Zhao Y, Kayed R. Lysine 63-linked ubiquitination of tau oligomers contributes to the pathogenesis of Alzheimer's disease. J Biol Chem. 2022 298(4):101766

Comment[Instrument]: Easy nLC1000 UHPLC-Q Exactive Orbitrap LC-MS system (Thermo Scientific)
Comment[GradientTime]: A 1-h linear gradient from 2% solvent A (0.1% formic acid in water) to 35% solvent B (0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile) was used for each LC-MS/MS run.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of two missed cleavages were allowed.
Comment[FractionationMethod]: Separation of PBS soluble and insoluble tau
